title: podman-compose not compatible with drives whose name contains "." or with simlinked directories
body:
### Issue Description

All my git projects are on an external SSD I'd named `M.2`. When running `podman-compose -p {target} up --build -d` from a project directory on this drive I'd get the following errors:
```
Copying config sha256:9ded92f6e58904cadd7fb6df8dcc8ca23cf034aba4c6640342c35f5ae8feffcd
Writing manifest to image destination
Error: statfs /Volumes/M.2/Projects/workspace/{project_directory}: no such file or directory
Error: no container with name or ID "install-npm_1" found: no such container
```
Renaming the drive to `M2` and moving the project directory to the internal drive solved the problem, but not until I was nearly bald from tearing my hair out.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. On a Mac, connect an external drive and rename it as something containing a dot (period).
2. Clone a git project which has Docker configuration enabled
3. Create a symbolic link to that directory & cd to it
4. Run `podman-compose -p {target} up --build -d` (where {target} is an alias for a Docker image)

### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: It turns out `podman-compose` doesn't like symlinks either.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
